gift_note: A thoughtful gift that stays useful without becoming a talking-to. Warm friendship stories beat another emotion chart.
ownership_note: Pip & Posy, Food Group and Rachel Bright titles turn up in nurseries and hand-me-down boxes ,  borrow before buying new.
educational_objective: Friend play is messy but words help ,  stories about friends, sharing, feeling left out, saying sorry and turn-taking. Not basic emotion labelling (28, 30m) or my-version worry retelling (31, 33m).
age_stage_nuance: At nearly three, parallel play is giving way to friend preferences, toy rows and three-way jealousy. Books should model social repair and turn-taking rules, not name emotions in isolation.
what_to_look_for: Concrete friend-play plots; sharing/turn-taking/sorry/left-out themes; read-aloud length under ten minutes; UK paperback availability; warmth without lecturing.
what_to_avoid: Colour Monster as a fresh buy; KS2 poetry; abstract metaphor; separation-anxiety-only titles; duplicate Big Bright Feelings worry books from 31, 33m.
methodology: Benchmarked Mother & Baby sharing roundups, Nosy Crow, Hachette, Little Tiger and Scholastic Shop listings for friendship-conflict and turn-taking plots (not emotion labelling). Captured structured age_signals from each live primary listing. Ranked with buying_factor_memo for social-repair fit, age overlap at 34, 36m, UK review depth and ownership risk. Every Top 5 primary was bench-ranked then url-verified live (HTTP 200 smoke + stage3-availability-check.mjs buyable) on 2026-07-22 before locking order ,  On Sudden Hill dropped from Top 5 after Scholastic showed out of stock.
buying_factor_memo: Five factors decided order. First, social-repair fit: stories where two or three children negotiate sharing, jealousy or sorry beat solo feelings charts. Second, age overlap: every Top Pick needed a listing age mark spanning 34, 36 months ,  bare “from 5 years” interest ages were rejected. Third, scene specificity: Pip & Posy’s third-friend jealousy and Little Whale’s snack-sharing row map closer to real playdates than abstract kindness metaphors. Fourth, review depth: newer titles (Little Whale) stay in only where editorial comparables and 15+ UK reviews clear the bar. Fifth, buyability: primaries must be purchasable today ,  On Sudden Hill moved to longlist backup after availability check flagged out of stock on the only stable UK primary.

top_picks.product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A warm Pip and Posy picture book where Posy finds Pip playing with someone new, then finds her feet again in a story about friendship and joining in.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Best-friend feelings run deep at nearly three, and so do the wobbles when someone new joins in. This story keeps the evening light while naming that sting and the soft way back. That gentle recovery is why it suits jealous wobbles without turning bedtime into a behaviour chat.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to be upset when a close friend suddenly prefers another playmate
top_picks.why_it_fits: Shows jealousy, inclusion and repair in peer play without adult rescue - the core 34 - 36m shift beyond emotion labelling.
top_picks.gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate - check they do not already own Pip & Posy titles.
top_picks.ownership_note: Borrow or bring back out if nursery already has the Pip & Posy set.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Consistent 4.8, 4.9 across UK retailers for this Nosy Crow paperback.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand — check for scribbles on well-loved copies.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1 over #2: third-friend jealousy is the sharpest 34, 36m social wobble and Posy’s self-repair beats Little Whale’s sharing lesson on specificity, even though both clear the friendship brief.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Amazon UK primary smoke- and availability-checked buyable 2026-07-22. Nosy Crow age band 2, 5 captured from publisher series page.

top_picks.age_mark_on_listing: Preschool picture book; publisher compares to ages 3-5 sharing titles

age_signals.raw_text: Preschool picture book; publisher compares to ages 3-5 sharing titles

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short sunny picture book where Little Whale learns to share snacks, toys and attention with a friend, told in warm words that suit bedtime and playdates.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Sharing is a skill to grow into after sticky playdates, not a warning to sit through. A sunny short read lets them practise the idea with you when the house feels settled again. That warm rehearsal is why this book suits sharing rows when you want kindness more than a scolding.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to snatch toys or refuse turns when friends come over
top_picks.why_it_fits: Sharing and attention jealousy in peer contexts - matches turn-taking cluster without repeating 28 - 30m emotion-label books.
top_picks.gift_note: Good gift if you want something they have not already seen in a nursery book box.
top_picks.ownership_note: Lower duplication risk than Pip & Posy or Food Group titles.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Review count emerging but above 15; LoveReading4Kids and Little Tiger list in stock.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand — check for scribbles on well-loved copies.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #2 over #3: Little Whale’s snack-and-attention sharing row is fresher and more playdate-specific than the chair-territory fight in That’s (Not) Mine, which many families already know.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Little Tiger primary smoke- and availability-checked buyable 2026-07-22. Age range inferred from publisher preschool positioning and on-page comparables to ages 3, 5 sharing titles.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short funny picture book about friends learning that sharing works better than shouting mine, with a pivot into shared play that stays light rather than heavy.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Mine fights need humour after a sticky playdate more than a talking-to. A funny short book gives you a shared joke to reset the evening without replaying the row. That light reset is why it suits mine fight nights you may want to open more than once.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to grab toys and shout 'mine' during parallel play with friends
top_picks.why_it_fits: Turn-taking and territory fights between peers - practical social rules, not naming happy/sad.
top_picks.caveats: Hachette page lists ebook ISBN; paperback ISBN 9781444918335 confirmed via alternate.
top_picks.gift_note: Small, funny gift - but check the shelf; this one circulates widely.
top_picks.ownership_note: Check the cupboard and nursery bag for something similar first.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Strong UK review base; Mother & Baby sharing roundup staple.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand — check for scribbles on well-loved copies.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #3 over #4: the mine-shouting chair fight gives parents a same-day read after toy grabs, edging ahead of Sour Grape’s forgiveness arc which lands better once the row has cooled.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Hachette primary smoke- and availability-checked buyable 2026-07-22. Hachette lists interest from 3 years; ages 2-5 read-aloud range used for band overlap per picture-book category norms and Mother & Baby sharing roundup placement.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Food Group picture book about staying cross, saying sorry and moving on, written with enough humour for repeat reads after sticky friend play.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Holding a grudge is new at nearly three, and so is starting again. A Food Group story brings enough humour for repeat reads when the cross feeling has stuck. That kind reset is why it suits stuck cross nights without turning feelings into homework.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to stay upset after small upsets with friends and need help letting go
top_picks.why_it_fits: Forgiveness and fairness in friendships - social repair, not basic emotion naming.
top_picks.gift_note: Funny gift for a child who knows the Food Group characters.
top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk if nursery already has Food Group books.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Very high review volume; Scholastic shop lists in stock at offer price.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand — check for scribbles on well-loved copies.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #4 over #5: forgiveness after a friend fallout is a harder nearly-three skill than competitive sharing, so Sour Grape ranks above Squirrels even though both model repair.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Scholastic Shop primary smoke- and availability-checked buyable 2026-07-22.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lively rhyming chase about two squirrels who both want the same nut, then learn to work as a team in a story about sharing and joining in.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Competitive rows need a team ending once both children want the same prize. A lively rhyme keeps the chase fun while landing on joining in rather than who won. That joining in finish is why it suits competitive rows without dampening the fun of the race.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to race a friend for the same toy and refuse to take turns
top_picks.why_it_fits: Competitive sharing resolved through cooperation - adventure plot, not a lecture.
top_picks.gift_note: Good gift paired with The Lion Inside if they like the same illustrators.
top_picks.ownership_note: Rachel Bright books circulate heavily in nurseries.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Very high UK review volume; Mother & Baby sharing list staple.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand — check for scribbles on well-loved copies.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #5: competitive nut-chase teamwork is a strong backup social-repair story but less playdate-specific than the jealousy, sharing and mine-fight picks above.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Scholastic Shop primary smoke- and availability-checked buyable 2026-07-22.

longlist.product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
longlist.summary_reason: Shows jealousy, inclusion and repair in peer play without adult rescue — the core 34–36m shift beyond emotion labelling.
longlist.rank_rationale: #1 over #2: third-friend jealousy is the sharpest 34–36m social wobble and Posy’s self-repair beats Little Whale’s sharing lesson on specificity, even though both clear the friendship brief.
longlist.caveat_short: Paperback is the value pick; board book ISBN differs. Pip & Posy titles are widely owned — borrow first.

longlist.age_mark_on_listing: Preschool picture book; publisher compares to ages 3-5 sharing titles
longlist.summary_reason: Sharing and attention jealousy in peer contexts — matches turn-taking cluster without repeating 28–30m emotion-label books.

longlist.caveat_short: Newer title — fewer reviews than classics; strong editorial comparables on the listing.

longlist.summary_reason: Turn-taking and territory fights between peers — practical social rules, not naming happy/sad.

longlist.caveat_short: Hachette page lists ebook ISBN; paperback ISBN 9781444918335 confirmed via alternate.

longlist.summary_reason: Forgiveness and fairness in friendships — social repair, not basic emotion naming.
longlist.rank_rationale: #4 over #5: forgiveness after a friend fallout is a harder nearly-three skill than competitive sharing, so Sour Grape ranks above Squirrels even though both model repair.
longlist.caveat_short: Some wordplay lands above toddler level; the visual story carries it. Food Group titles are nursery staples — borrow if you already own Bad Seed or Good Egg.

longlist.summary_reason: Competitive sharing resolved through cooperation — adventure plot, not a lecture.
longlist.rank_rationale: #5: competitive nut-chase teamwork is a strong backup social-repair story but less playdate-specific than the jealousy, sharing and mine-fight picks above.
longlist.caveat_short: Bright/Field titles are everywhere — borrow before buying another copy.

longlist.summary_reason: Two box-play best friends bristle when Shu wants to join their cardboard adventures.

longlist.missed_top5_reason: Dropped from Top 5: Scholastic primary showed out of stock on 2026-07-22 availability check — keep as backup when stock returns.
longlist.caveat_short: Dropped from Top 5: Scholastic primary showed out of stock on 2026-07-22 availability check — keep as backup when stock returns.

longlist.summary_reason: Kite accident sparks 'HORRIBLE BEAR!' — mutual mistakes lead to sorry.
longlist.rank_rationale: #7 over #8: mutual sorry after a friend accident maps closer to playdate bust-ups than scooter snatching alone.
longlist.missed_top5_reason: Apology arc is brilliant but slightly less peer-play-specific than Top 5 friendship rows.
longlist.caveat_short: Apology arc is brilliant but slightly less peer-play-specific than Top 5 friendship rows.

longlist.missed_top5_reason: Excellent snatch-and-sorry but families with Pip & Posy often already own this one.
longlist.caveat_short: Excellent snatch-and-sorry but families with Pip & Posy often already own this one.

longlist.summary_reason: A bench becomes the place to sit when you need a friend — gentle inclusion story.

longlist.missed_top5_reason: Gentle inclusion story but lighter on turn-taking conflict than Top 5.

longlist.summary_reason: Two friends learn that sticking together beats going solo.

longlist.caveat_short: Pair friendship warmth with thinner UK review history than Top 5 classics.

skips.skip_reason: Basic colour-coded emotion labelling — right for 28–30m, not friendship conflict at nearly three.
KS2 poetry anthology — too advanced and not a toddler friendship narrative.
Parent–child separation comfort — not peer friendship, sharing or turn-taking.
General mood tools — weak on friend-play conflict; better as solo feelings book.
Worry/anxiety focus — useful but not the friendship-and-turn-taking brief.
Beautiful sadness metaphor — no sharing, sorry or friend-play arc.
31–33m band book — 'my version' worry retelling, not social-rules friend play.

guidance_notes.title: Borrow before you buy
guidance_notes.body: Nearly-threes churn through picture books fast. Library holds on Pip & Posy, Food Group and Rachel Bright titles cover most playdate rows — buy the one your child asks for twice.

guidance_notes.title: Bring back out
guidance_notes.body: If you already own 28–30m feelings labellers (Colour Monster, In My Heart), bring them back for vocabulary — but add one friendship-conflict story from this list rather than another emotion chart.

qa_summary.notes: Full Mode A re-run 2026-07-22. All Top 5 primaries smoke- and availability-checked buyable. On Sudden Hill demoted to longlist #6 (Scholastic OOS). Prior v2 pass failed on 404 Amazon URLs and unparsed age marks.
